title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · Linux Stakeholder Mission
summary: A small organization needs to recover a failed worker without losing its queued data. Deliver storage, archive, restore, unit, log, readiness, and rollback evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Service and Backup Recovery Runbook with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 3. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Service Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 6. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.
